seoTitle: Use ClickUp for Software Development: Sprint Setup
metaDescription: Set up ClickUp for agile software development with sprint folders, story point fields, Git integrations, and dashboards for tracking team velocity.
intro: ClickUp's Software Development suite gives engineering teams a way to run sprints, track backlogs, and connect Git activity without leaving their task manager. This guide covers building a sprint structure, adding custom fields for story points and bug severity, and linking GitHub or GitLab so pull requests update task status automatically. It's built for teams already running some form of agile process.

steps.heading: Set up Sprint Folders
steps.body: Use ClickUp's Sprint Folders to automate weekly sprint creation and roll spillover tasks into the next cycle.

steps.heading: Add development-specific custom fields
steps.body: Create fields for story points, bug severity, and epic tracking so tasks carry the metadata your team needs.

steps.heading: Connect your Git provider
steps.body: Go to Integrations settings to securely connect GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket to your workspace.

steps.heading: Automate status changes from pull requests
steps.body: Build workflow rules that move tasks from In Progress to In Review when a pull request opens.

steps.heading: Track work across views
steps.body: Switch between backlog lists, Kanban boards, and Workload charts to manage priorities and capacity.

steps.heading: Monitor velocity with dashboards
steps.body: Add burn-down and cumulative flow widgets to a shared dashboard so the team can track sprint progress.

faq.question: Does ClickUp integrate with GitHub for status updates?
faq.answer: Yes, you can connect GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket and build automation rules that shift task status when a pull request is opened.

faq.question: Can I track story points in ClickUp?
faq.answer: Yes, custom fields let you add story points, bug severity, and epic tracking to your tasks.

faq.question: Is ClickUp suitable for running Scrum sprints?
faq.answer: Sprint Folders and board views are built to support Scrum-style sprint planning and backlog management.

faq.question: Can ClickUp Brain summarize commit history?
faq.answer: ClickUp Brain can be used to compile code changes into formatted documentation and release notes.
